name: The Blessed
description: Danu, the Great Mother, provides. We need only learn her secrets: the names by which the trees call each other; the mark made with redberry juice to ward off impure spirits; the language of the wolves. A thousand such secrets Danu keeps, to share with only her true children. Her Blessed.
statsNote: Assign these scores to your stats: +2, +1, +1, +0, +0, -1

backgrounds.label: Initiate
backgrounds.description: Stonetop has long been home to a sacred order, keepers of the old ways and speakers for Danu. You are one such initiate, the most gifted in generations. You gain the Rites of the Land move.

choices.label: There are other initiates in Stonetop, serving the goddess and the village. They aid you as followers— see the Initiates of Danu insert. Who are they?

options.label: Enfys, your acolyte, beloved by birds
options.subtitle: Bird-wise, innocent, magical, well-informed

options.label: Gwendyl, your mentor, a talented healer
options.subtitle: Herb-wise, gossipy, tireless, healer, magical

options.label: Olwin, your anointed lover, seer of fates
options.subtitle: Fates-wise, beautiful, passionate, magical

options.label: Seren the Eldest, wise and hard as winter
options.subtitle: Exceptional, story-wise, insightful, frail, magical

backgrounds.label: Raised by Wolves
backgrounds.description:
Maybe not by wolves, but you grew up in the wild. Beasts of land and air were your siblings. The sighing wind taught you language. The trees and rocks were your home. Were you one of the Forest Folk? Abandoned or orphaned? Lured into the Wood?

Regardless, you get the Trackless Step move. Also, when you Forage, you have advantage.

For some reason, you've made yourself known to Stonetop and perhaps you even call it home. But the ways of the humans are still strange to you. Once per session, when your wild ways offend or alienate you from someone, mark XP.

backgrounds.label: Vessel
backgrounds.description: A seed of Danu's power has taken root in your soul. Perhaps it has always been there and only recently sprouted. Or maybe it was planted in you during some portentous event. Take the Danu's Grasp move. When you would spend 1 Stock from your sacred pouch, you may choose to lose 2d4 HP instead.

instincts.description: To find beauty, in even the ugliest things.
To remain unmoved, to be cold as winter.
To help others grow, learn, or improve.
To protect the natural world.
To honor the spirits and give them their due.

specialPossessions.pickNote: Pick 2, in addition to your sacred pouch

options.label: Sacred pouch (magical)
options.description:
Doesn't take up space in your inventory. It can hold up to 3 Stock (sacred herbs, powders, stones, pigments, chalks, clay, and so forth). Each time you gain an even-numbered level, your pouch can hold +1 Stock. When anyone but you looks inside your sacred pouch and touches the materials therein, the Stock is ruined.

When you have a few days of downtime in familiar terrain, you may replenish your Stock.

When you Forage, you can produce Stock instead of provisions.

options.label: an heirloom made just for you

choiceGroups.heading: What remarkable trait does it possess?

options.label: It cannot be cut, torn, or burned by any natural means.
Unless someone is specifically searching for your pouch, they will ignore its presence.
So long as the pouch is sealed, nothing within can be detected or found by magic, nor can anything within escape or affect the outside world.
Unnatural and unclean creatures cannot bear to touch it.
Apiary
options.description: beeswax, candles (close, area, lasts ~1 hr), honey, ◇ bee smokers, ◇ hat & veils, etc.

options.label: Collected offerings
options.description: (○○○ uses) Expend a use to produce something valuable to a spirit of the wild. Restore 1 use each season.

options.label: Goat herd
options.description: milk, cheese, pelts, meat, blood, horn, wool, etc. Each season, 1 in 4 chance of having a bezoar (swallow it to cure poison).

options.label: Herb garden
options.description: shears, mortars & pestles, herbs, seeds, remedies, mild poisons, ◇ spades, etc. Each spring, d4 uses of bendis root (reach, area, burns ~1 hr, fumes repel perversions of nature).

options.label: Mastiffs
options.description: 2-3 followers (alert, keen-nosed, fierce, overprotective); HP 6; Damage d6 (hand, grabby); Instinct: to bark & threaten; Cost: affection.

lore.title: The Earth Mother
lore.description: Danu has long been revered by all peoples, though not always worshipped or served by priests. In Stonetop's Pavilion of the Gods, Danu's shrine is... (choose 1)

options.description: ... loved, well-used, dripping with offerings and petitions.
... little more than a token of respect, for her holy places are anywhere but here.
... given wide berth by most, and approached only with care and propitiation.
... neglected and all but forgotten, except by a few.

lore.title: Offerings to Danu
lore.description: What do the folk of Stonetop leave as offerings? (choose 2–3)

options.description: Fruits of harvest
Figurines/effigies
Whisky/spirits
Salt/crystals
Pure rain water
Metal nails/tools
Blood/burnt flesh
Incense/sage bark

movesNote.startingMovesNote: You start with Spirit Tongue, Call the Spirits, 1 from your Background, and 1 of your choice.

name: The Fox
description: The elders tell a story about Fox, who knows lots of tricks, and Hedgehog, who knows one: how to curl up into a ball when there's danger. Fox can't eat Hedgehog when he's all curled up, so in the story Fox goes hungry. But you're not that Fox, and this is no story. You want that Hedgehog? Go get a knife.
statsNote: Assign these scores to your stats: +2, +1, +1, +0, +0, -1

backgrounds.label: The Natural
backgrounds.description:
You grew up around here, and always picked things up quickly. Reading and numbers, sure, but more. Hide and seek. Throwing stones. Climbing. Fighting. Whatever you tried, you were good at it. As good as anyone else, if not better.

Sure, you've got a reputation for bending the rules. Playing dirty. But why play if you don't play to win, right? And who do they come to when there's a problem needs solving? You, that's who.

When you Seek Insight, you may roll +INT instead of +WIS and add "What opportunity does no one else see?" to the list of possible questions.

backgrounds.label: A Life of Crime
backgrounds.description:
You're new to Stonetop, having left behind a... colorful past. How did you get into that life? Why and how did you get out? Who and what do you leave behind?

Regardless, these people have taken you in. Time to lead an honest life, right?

You start with either Burgle or Light Fingers (your choice) as an extra move, and either burglar tools or a hidden stash (your choice) as an additional special possession. Go mark them now.

backgrounds.label: The Prodigal Returned
backgrounds.description:
You left long ago, travelling far and living by your wits. Why did you leave? What deeds do you boast of, and which do you regret?

You always longed to return to Stonetop, and return you have. You're a bit of a celebrity now, and you've got friends (or close enough) strewn about the known world.

When you declare that you know someone outside of Stonetop, someone who can help, name them and roll +CHA: on a 10+, yeah, they can help (tell us why they're willing); on a 7-9, they can help but pick 1 from the list below; on a 6-, the GM chooses 1 and then some.

instincts.description: To feel guilty, to try to do right.
To chafe against rules, expectations, obligations.
To enjoy yourself and avoid hardship.
To impress others, to build a name for yourself.
To deceive, misdirect, outthink.

origin.region: Lygos or some other point south

options.label: Burglar's kit
options.description: picks, files, snippets, wire, ◇ prybars, ◇ hacksaws, ◇ a lantern (○○○○○ hours, close, area), ◇ a grappling hook, etc.

options.label: Carpenter's tools
options.description: chisels, files, nails, pitch, ◇ prybars, ◇ saws, ○○ firkins, barrels, etc.

options.label: Distillery
options.description: skins of fine whisky (grants advantage to Persuade), copper tubes, malt, firkins, stills, barrels, etc.

options.label: Hidden stash
options.description: each use produces valuables worth a purse of silvers (Value 2).

options.label: Mummer's kit
options.description: juggling balls, whirlybird seeds, motley, ribbons, bells, ◇ puppets, ◇ a fiddle, etc.

options.label: Scribe's tools
options.description: parchment, ink, pigments, vials, quills, ◇ a notebook, etc.

options.label: Trade contacts
options.description: small amounts of salt, glass, silk, spice, medicinal herbs, pigments, ivory, etc.

lore.title: There Was That Time You…
lore.description: Someone like you gets into all sorts of trouble, whether you mean to or not. Mix and match the following to come up with a couple of your more memorable adventures. (choose 1 per tale)

options.description: … got lost in the Great Wood.
… got lost in the Flats.
… got lost in the Steplands.
… got lost in Ferrier's Fen.
… got lost in the Foothills.
… got lost in the Huffel Peaks.
… were on watch when the crinwin raided.
… dared each other to explore the Ruined Tower.
… managed to rile up a small band of Hillfolk.
… braved the Labyrinth, just a little.
… stole that crazy old man's book.
… went poking about the old Barrow Mounds.
… running for your life from something.
… landing a well-placed blow.
… interrupting a strange, creepy gathering.
… stumbling on a beast, bigger'n anything.
… with a sack full of treasure.
… getting someone to fight them for you.
… face to face with a ghost/Fae/demon.
… finding those strange old runes.
… getting to know that fine-looking fellow/lady/person/couple.

lore.title: But All You've Got Left to Show for It Is…

options.description: … a story no one believes.
… a nasty scar; wanna see?
… the occasional nightmare.
… this map with runes no one can read.
… this key that opens who-knows-what.

movesNote.startingMovesNote: You start with Ambush OR Skill at Arms; Danger Sense OR Perceptive; and 1 of your choice.

name: The Heavy
description: These are good people. Hard-working, honest. They look out for each other. But sometimes, looking out for each other ain't enough. Sometimes, good people need someone to stick up for them. Someone who's not afraid to get a little bloody. To get heavy. Yeah, someone like you.
statsNote: Assign these scores to your stats: +2, +1, +1, +0, +0, -1

backgrounds.label: Sheriff
backgrounds.description:
You keep order in Stonetop and protect it from outside threats. It might not be anything official, but everyone knows you've got a cool head and the weight to back up your words.

When you bark an order or warning, roll +CHA: on a 7+, they must choose 1:

  • do what you say
  • dig in/take cover/flee
    • or
  • attack you
On a 10+, you can sense which one they're about to do and act first if you like; gain advantage if you do.

backgrounds.label: Blood-Soaked Past
backgrounds.description:
You left behind a life of violence and a name mothers used to scare their children. For whatever reason, the people of Stonetop took you (back?) in and treat you like one of their own.

When you Persuade using violence or threats against someone who knows your black reputation, you can roll +STR instead of +CHA. Also, if you take the Formidable move, you can choose to roll +CON instead of +CHA.

When you fight to kill without mercy or hesitation, you deal +1d4 damage.

backgrounds.label: Storm-Marked
backgrounds.description:
You've been touched by Tor (Rain-maker, Thunderhead, Slayer-of-Beasts!) and bear runic markings similar to those etched into the Stone. When did the marks manifest? Are they a symbol of your strength, speed, and courage? Or their source?

You start with the Storm Markings major arcanum. Mark one of the boxes on the front of the Storm Markings sheet, and describe here the time you were struck by lightning and walked away unharmed.

instincts.description: To avoid (further) bloodshed or violence.
To maintain your dignity, to demand respect.
To act without thought to the consequences.
To stick your nose in where it's unwelcome.
To solve problems by force.

origin.region: Lygos or some other point south

options.label: Distillery
options.description: ○○ uses: skins of fine whisky (grants advantage to Persuade), copper tubes, malt, firkins, stills, barrels, etc.

options.label: Chirurgeon's tools
options.description: catgut, straps, bandages, tubes, poultices, willow bark, ◇ bonesaws, etc.

options.label: Husbandry tools
options.description: brushes, muzzles, collars, feed, ◇ whips, ◇ bridles, etc. Gain advantage to Persuade domestic beasts (livestock, dogs, etc.).

options.label: Smithy
options.description: (or access to it): iron goods, ingots, thick gloves, ◇ tongs, ◇ bellows, an anvil, etc.

options.label: Stoneworker's tools
options.description: chisels, drills, ◇ prybars, ◇ spikes, ◇ block & tackles, wheelbarrow, etc.

lore.title: Just About Everyone Here Talks About the Time You…

options.description: … drove off a thunder drake that got too close to town.
… killed that hagr in the Foothills.
… slew a dozen crinwin in one battle.
… tossed those adventurers out of town.
… bested Ivan, the scariest bandit in Brennan's gang, the Claws.
… dragged yourself (and another?) into town, bleeding from a dozen wounds.
… were struck by lightning and woke up covered in these marks.

lore.title: But Folks Are Less Keen to Discuss…

options.description: … the look in your eye when you spilled all that blood.
… those hard cases who showed up looking for you.
… the shouting matches between you and your love.
… the time you spent as one of Brennan's Claws.
… what happened to Urbgen, even if he did have it coming.
… your uncontrollable seizures, where you claw those weird marks in the dirt.

lore.title: What Keeps You Up at Night?

options.description: That thrice-damned temper of yours.
The worry that someone's coming after you.
The feeling that the crinwin are getting bolder.
Wondering what Brennan's up to, now that he's the marshal of Marshedge.
Dark visions of things moving in the earth, restless, whispering, and hungry.
The question of who'll look after your family when you get yourself killed.
The worry that they'll all learn the truth about you, sooner or later.

movesNote.startingMovesNote: You start with Dangerous, Hard to Kill, and either Armored OR Uncanny Reflexes.

name: The Judge
description: Look here at this little town, this candleflame in the darkness. Its very existence is an act of courage and faith. And Aratis has charged you to keep it: to settle its disputes; to chronicle its tales; to defend it from darkness and ruin. Take up your hammer, Judge. Your town needs you.
statsNote: Assign these scores to your stats: +2, +1, +1, +0, +0, -1

backgrounds.label: Legacy
backgrounds.description:
You are the latest in a long line of Judges—born here, apprenticed to the prior Judge, and charged with the passing of the mantle. The Chronicle is a rich repository of lore, but there's no index, so good luck finding anything.

When you Know Things about the people or history of Stonetop, you have advantage.

When you spend days, weeks, or months poring over the Chronicle, ask the GM and the GM will tell you what you learn in that time.

backgrounds.label: Missionary
backgrounds.description:
You are part of a larger order of Judges, sent here to protect the flickering flame of civilization. The Chronicle is relatively new; your position in town is far from certain.

When you call upon the Judge of another steading for aid or information, they are oathbound to give it. You are likewise oathbound to support them.

You have an aviary in addition to your usual choice of special possessions. When you send a message via trained bird, the GM will tell you if and when you receive a response, and what it says.

backgrounds.label: Prophet
backgrounds.description:
The line of Judges was broken long ago, the Chronicle lost or fallen into ruin. Aratis has called you personally to her service through dreams, omens, and visions. Some in town resent the authority you've assumed.

When you spend a few days communing with Aratis about a threat facing Stonetop or civilization as a whole, roll +WIS: on a 7+, Aratis reveals the course of action she would have you take; on a 10+, you also hold 2 Sanction. While acting on her orders, spend 1 Sanction to add +1 to a roll you just made.

instincts.description: To increase your status or influence.
To disregard emotion or sentiment.
To seek a path that makes everyone happy.
To strictly adhere to rules and traditions.
To judge quickly and without doubt.

origin.region: Lygos or some other southern town

specialPossessions.pickNote: Pick 1, in addition to your symbol of authority and scribe's kit

options.label: Symbol of authority
options.description: your official badge and symbol; recognized throughout the Manmarch.

options.label: Scribe's kit
options.description: parchment, ink, pigments, vials, quills, ◇ a notebook, etc.

options.label: Aviary
options.description: a building with trained birds to carry messages. When you send a message by trained bird, the GM will tell you when (or if) you receive a reply, and what it says.

options.label: Carpenter's tools
options.description: chisels, files, nails, pitch, ◇ prybars, ◇ saws, ○○ firkins, barrels, etc.

options.label: Engineer's tools
options.description: measuring string, stakes, levels, plumb bobs, ◇ compasses, ◇ drawing tools, plans, etc.

options.label: Smithy
options.description: (or access to it): iron goods, ingots, thick gloves, ◇ tongs, ◇ bellows, an anvil, etc.

lore.title: The Chronicle
lore.description: The Judge of Aratis is charged with maintaining the Chronicle, a history of the community, its people, their knowledge, and their traditions. It is more than a mere book; it is a physical place. On the plus side, it… (choose 3)

options.description: … is a sturdy vault from the time of the Makers.
… has plenty of room to grow.
… is hidden underground.
… has but one entrance, magically sealed.
… bears minor magics to preserve its contents.
… is warded against spirits and magic.
… includes your living quarters & office.
… sits on the outskirts, near the Old Wall.
… is cramped, chaotic, and overflowing.
… is little more than a crude cellar.
… seems to be haunted.
… contains a few dangerous artifacts.

lore.title: The Lawkeeper
lore.description: Her Judges say that Aratis has been with humanity since they first stacked one stone upon another and called it home. In Stonetop's Pavilion of the Gods, Aratis's shrine is… (pick 1)

options.description: … a hub of the community, a place of frequent rites, petitions, and celebrations.
… used only on high holidays, for each home keeps its own shrine above the hearth.
… neglected by most, tended only by you and a handful of believers.
… a grim place of judgement and punishment, shunned by all but her chosen.
… newly established, cramped and spare.

lore.title: Of Her True Disciples, Aratis Demands…

options.description: … truth, honesty, and forthrightness.
… hospitality, freely given to all who ask for it.
… the punishment of thieves & oathbreakers.
… adherence to strict rules of diet and dress.
… respect for authority, property, and rank.

movesNote.startingMovesNote: You start with Censure, Chronicler of Stonetop, plus 2 more of your choice.

name: The Lightbearer
description: Imagine yourself and your kin in a cave lit by a single torch, entranced by shadow puppet stories. Imagine realizing there is a greater truth, and stepping out of the cave into the true Light of day. Would you not bring that Light back into the darkness, to set your people free?
statsNote: Assign these scores to your stats: +2, +1, +1, +0, +0, -1

backgrounds.label: Auspicious Birth
backgrounds.description:
You were born in Stonetop, and that birth was marked by the God of Light. You were born during an eclipse, perhaps, or under the light of a bright new star? Maybe you bear a sun-shaped birthmark?

Whatever the sign, your connection to Helior was clear early on. You've a place of honor in Stonetop, though it'd be a lie to say you don't make some uneasy.

When one of your moves has you mark a debility, you may mark this background's circle instead, to no ill effect. Clear it when you Make Camp or Convalesce.

backgrounds.label: Itinerant Mystic
backgrounds.description:
They think of you as a self-important kook who comes through now and again, speaking in riddles and playing tricks with the light. Sure, they know there's something holy about you, but it's not like you're a priest or anything. Priests talk sense.

When you go off a-wandering, hold 1 Enigma if you're gone for days, 2 if you're gone for weeks, or 3 if you're gone for months. At the very start of play, hold 3 Enigma. Spend Enigma 1-for-1 to: return from your wandering exactly when and where you are needed, fully Outfitted; Know Things as if you rolled a 10+; or have What You Need to produce an oddly specific yet mundane item of Value 1 or less.

backgrounds.label: Soul on Fire
backgrounds.description:
You once led a worldly life, full of fear and doubt, base pleasures and petty grudges. But something happened. Injury, illness, a brush with death. Or just a moment of such profound misery and self-loathing that you thought you could go no further.

There, in the dark, Helior's light shined upon you, igniting in your soul, lifting you and filling you with a profound sense of purpose.

When you Persuade a group by preaching charity, mercy, and hope and roll a 7+, aside from the usual effect, choose 1:

  • your name and your message spread
  • someone approaches you, now or later, eager to know more.

instincts.description: To go without so that others are better off.
To inspire others in the face of adversity.
To bring relief or comfort, to give second chances.
To spread the glory and worship of Helior.
To refuse to suffer an injustice or a lesser evil.

origin.region: Lygos or some other point south

options.label: Apiary
options.description: beeswax, candles (close, area, lasts ~1 hr), honey, ◇ bee smokers, ◇ hat & veils, etc.

options.label: Books & scrolls
options.description: ○○○○○ uses: a collection of religious texts and sacred histories. Mark a use to Know Things about Helior, the Sun-Worshippers, or their lore as if you rolled a 10+.

options.label: Chandlery
options.description: tallow, wax, wicks, dye, ◇ molds; candles, wick-holders, holy candles (close, area, ~1 hr), lanterns, etc.

options.label: Distillery
options.description: ○○ uses: skins of fine spirits (Value 1; grants advantage to Persuade when shared), copper tubes, malt, firkins, stills, barrels, etc.

options.label: Glassworks
options.description: sand, ash, ◇ crucibles, ◇ blowpipes; bottles, flasks, lenses, lantern glass, etc.

options.label: Holy relics
options.description: ○○○ uses: sacred objects of Helior's worship. Expend a use to add +1 to a roll involving the god's favor or power. Restore 1 use each season.

options.label: Luthier's tools
options.description: gut strings, pegs, wood, varnish, ◇ chisels, ◇ clamps; instruments, repairs, etc.

lore.title: The Worship of Helior Is…
lore.description: You are the appointed servant of Helior the Daybringer, god of the sun and light, beacon of hope and mercy. (choose 1)

options.description: … ancient, widespread, and well-known.
… most common in Lygos and the south.
… a new thing, still unheard of by many.
… an old thing, forgotten by most.
… widely persecuted.
… solemn hymns.
… serene meditation.
… joyful song.
… ascetic denial.
… fervent dancing.
… formal ceremonies.
… drugs & intoxicants.
… pain & sacrifice.

lore.title: In Stonetop's Pavilion of the Gods, Helior's Shrine Has…

options.description: … the place of highest honor, even if Tor is more popular.
… been well-tended and given due respect.
… recently been restored/established, perhaps by you.
… seen better days, for certain.
… lived long ago, a figure of legend.
… was martyred for their faith.
… died facing a mighty sorcerer or demon.
… wrote many works of sublime beauty.
… faced one of the Things Below.
… died in their bed, peacefully.
… ascended bodily into the heavens.
… was reincarnated—as you.
… through years of study and devotion.
… when your predecessor passed them on.
… suddenly, at a moment of great need.
… after a visitation from Helior or one of his servants.
… when you first laid eyes upon the _______.

options.label: Bath of Healing Light
options.description: Focus your light on a patient; pick 2: regain 5 HP (twice OK), clear a debility (twice OK), stabilize a wound, or recover from a minor condition. Reduced: pick only 1. Empowered: additional healing options including 10 HP or curing dire afflictions.

options.label: Blinding Light (ongoing)
options.description: Your light blazes; anyone in range who looks is temporarily blinded, others must avert their eyes. You are unaffected. Reduced: flares only for a moment. Empowered: your allies are unaffected.

options.label: Cleansing Light
options.description: Dispels magical effects within range; potent lasting magics are merely suppressed. Reduced: only weak effects dispelled. Empowered: becomes ongoing — all magic created in or entering range is dispelled.

options.label: Cold Light of Day (ongoing)
options.description: All in your light appears as it really is — illusions, shapeshifting, and disguise fail. Reduced: lasts only briefly. Empowered: illusions dispelled; shapeshifters momentarily stunned.

options.label: Dancing Light (ongoing)
options.description: Your light floats free, moving anywhere you can see; you can change its shape or color. Reduced: range reduced by one step. Empowered: you can channel another Invocation through Dancing Light.

options.label: Go Back to the Shadow
options.description: Spirits of darkness in your light take 2d8 damage (ignores armor); at 0 HP they are banished or returned to their tether. Reduced: 1d8 only. Empowered: spirit utterly destroyed, or banished with its tether destroyed.

options.label: Hold Back the Darkness (ongoing)
options.description: Spirits and creatures of darkness cannot approach; cowardly ones flee; those forced in deal damage with disadvantage. Reduced: requires unbroken litany of prayers. Empowered: forced entities become vulnerable to mundane weapons.

options.label: Moth to a Flame (ongoing)
options.description: Name a creature type; they follow your light slowly. Effect ends if they take damage. Reduced: brief, or only some affected. Empowered: effect persists briefly after first damage; ends on second hit.

options.label: Terrible as the Dawn (ongoing)
options.description: Name a creature type; your light fills them with dread, causing recoil and flight. Reduced: all mortal creatures including allies affected. Empowered: even the brave must cower or flee.

options.label: Warmth of the Sun (ongoing)
options.description: You and your allies in your light are free of fear, doubt, and extreme cold. Reduced: only one person protected. Empowered: also protects from necromantic and life-draining effects.

movesNote.startingMovesNote: You start with Consecrated Flame and Invoke the Sun God, plus 1 more of your choice.

name: The Marshal
description: Hoping for peace isn't enough. Trouble always comes knocking. And that's why we need you: to run the drills, to man the towers, to take charge when things get bad. To be cold enough to send your neighbors to a sure death in order to keep Stonetop safe. That's the job, Marshal. You up for it?
statsNote: Assign these scores to your stats: +2, +1, +1, +0, +0, -1

backgrounds.label: Scion
backgrounds.description:
You grew up here, descended from a long line. Some of the biggest names in Stonetop's past are perched in your family tree. Everyone in the village takes your authority as a given, and your crew is a well-established institution in town.

You start with the Veteran Crew move, in addition to your usual moves. Go mark it now.

When you create your Crew, they automatically have the respected tag (in addition to your usual picks, and any you get from Veteran Crew).

backgrounds.label: Penitent
backgrounds.description:
Before you came here, you led a band of ne'er-do-wells: bandits, raiders, or bloody-handed mercenaries. But something changed. A moment of truth led you and your followers—some of them at least—to leave that life behind. And for whatever reason, the people of Stonetop took you in.

When you draw on your bloody past to Know Things, you may roll +STR instead of +INT. If you do, the GM will ask you who you wronged back then or who might still hold a grudge. Answer them now.

When you create your Crew, they automatically have the warriors tag (in addition to your usual picks).

backgrounds.label: Luminary
backgrounds.description:
You're a natural leader—your words inspire, your plans win the day, your deeds are recounted far and wide. Are you touched by the gods? Does ancient blood flow in your veins? Or are you simply the champion that Stonetop needs in these trying times?

You start with the We Happy Few move, in addition to your usual moves. Go mark it now.

When you create your Crew, they automatically have the devoted tag (in addition to your usual picks).

instincts.description: To take charge and throw your weight around.
To keep everyone safe, to agonize over decisions.
To take on ever more responsibility.
To keep your word, to follow a moral code.
To do whatever it takes to win or survive.

origin.region: Lygos or some other point south

options.label: Chirurgeon's tools
options.description: catgut, straps, bandages, tubes, poultices, willow bark, ◇ bonesaws, etc.

options.label: Distillery
options.description: ○○ uses: skins of fine whisky (grants advantage to Persuade), copper tubes, malt, firkins, stills, barrels, etc.

options.label: Engineer's tools
options.description: measuring string, stakes, levels, plumb bobs, ◇ compasses, ◇ drawing tools, plans, etc.

options.label: Personal symbol
options.description: a banner, insignia, or seal that marks you as the Marshal of Stonetop. Recognized throughout the Manmarch.

options.label: Scribe's tools
options.description: parchment, ink, pigments, vials, quills, ◇ a notebook, etc.

lore.title: The Last Time the Militia Saw Serious Action, It Was…

options.description: … to repel a nighttime raid by crinwin from the Great Wood.
… to drive off bandits who'd taken up near the Ruined Tower.
… to fend off Hillfolk pursuing a blood feud.
… against Brennan and his Claws, before they settled in Marshedge.
… to face a brutish hagr, come down from the Foothills to wreak havoc.
… to hunt down beasts (wolves, drakes, or bears maybe?) who'd been preying on the village.

lore.title: Answer At Least 3 of the Following

options.description: When exactly did it happen?
Who lost their life, and who mourns them?
Who from Stonetop was maimed, and how?
Who saved the day, and how?
How did the enemy get away, and whom do you still blame for it?
Who comported themselves with honor?
What's been bugging you about it ever since?
What's got you even more worried now?

instincts: To bicker, infight, and hold grudges
To hew to tradition and superstition
costs: Risks taken, by you, to help them
Wealth gained for themselves or Stonetop

inventory.label: Bow & iron arrows (near, x piercing, ○ low ammo, ○ all out)

movesNote.startingMovesNote: You start with Crew, Logistics, any moves from your Background, and 1 move of your choice.

name: The Ranger
description: Your true home is out there. Away from the Old Roads, in the wild places, where you've faced storm and beast alike. But unknown forces are at work beyond the Ringwall, and you fear for your kith and kin. These are strange times. Guide them, ranger, and keep them safe when darkness falls.
statsNote: Assign these scores to your stats: +2, +1, +1, +0, +0, -1

backgrounds.label: Mighty Hunter
backgrounds.description:
You are a hunter of the Great Wood, the best the town has seen in generations. You know every part of the Wood within a two-day march.

You start with both the Expert Tracker move and the Stalker move. Go mark them now.

backgrounds.label: Wide Wanderer
backgrounds.description:
You have travelled much of the known world and perhaps parts beyond. Add each of the following to the Neighbors list in the Stonetop playbook, choosing 1 trait for each:

  • Ennis (from Marshedge)
  • Shahar (from Gordin's Delve)
  • Yannic (from the Hillfolk)>/li>
  • Tovia (from Lygos)
  • Sasca (from the northern Manmarch)

You start with the Mental Map move. Mark it now.

When you Know Things about the wider world, you can roll +WIS instead of +INT. When you arrive somewhere you've visited before (your call), tell the GM when you were last here, and the GM will tell you how it's changed.

backgrounds.label: Beast-Bonded
backgrounds.description:
You grew up civilized, but your soul is bound to a beast of the wild. You're closer to it than to any man or woman. How did this bond come about? How long ago?

Regardless, you start with the Animal Companion move. Go mark it now.

When you focus on your animal companion for a few moments, you can use any of the actions you've marked, no matter the distance between you. Mark 1 action at 1st level, then another at 3rd, 5th, 7th, and 9th.

instincts.description: To test yourself, to experience new things.
To refuse help and push others away.
To value beasts and natural places over people.
To be stubborn, to persist.
To marvel at beauty, magnificence, splendor.

origin.region: Lygos or some other point south

specialPossessions.pickNote: Pick 2, in addition to your composite bow

options.label: Composite bow
options.description: near/far, 2 piercing. A powerful, well-crafted bow that can reach far and punch through armor.

options.label: Distillery
options.description: ○○ uses: skins of fine whisky (grants advantage to Persuade), copper tubes, malt, firkins, stills, barrels, etc.

options.label: Hideouts
options.description: ○○○ uses: 2-3 stocked shelters in the wild, each a safe place to sleep and cache supplies. When you arrive at a hideout, mark a use to restore Supplies or Ammo.

options.label: Hounds
options.description: 2-3 followers (alert, keen-nosed, fierce, overprotective); HP 6; Damage d6 (hand, grabby); Instinct: to bark & threaten; Cost: affection.

options.label: Husbandry tools
options.description: brushes, muzzles, collars, feed, ◇ whips, ◇ bridles, etc. Gain advantage to Persuade domestic beasts (livestock, dogs, etc.).

options.label: Lay of the land
options.description: ○○○ uses: your intimate knowledge of the terrain within two days' march. Expend a use to ask the GM a question about what's out there; the GM answers honestly.

options.label: Trapping gear
options.description: snares, traps, lures, bait, stakes, ◇ a net, etc. When you spend an evening setting traps, the GM will tell you what you catch by morning.

lore.title: Something Wicked This Way Comes
lore.description: You know firsthand that trouble is out there, and like it or not, one of these days the folk of Stonetop are going to have to face it. What is it that you're so worried about? (choose 1)

options.description: A dark, unwholesome presence lurking in the Great Wood.
A strange, furtive figure seen near the Ruined Tower.
Something big & savage stalking the northern foothills.
Whatever's made the lizard-like suarachan of Ferrier's Fen so bold.
That of which the Hillfolk refuse to speak.

lore.title: Answer At Least 3 of the Following

options.description: What, exactly, do you think it is?
What did you see, and how close did you have to get to see it?
Whom or what have you lost to it?
What did it leave behind?
What do you think it wants?
Who refuses to believe you?
Who can tell you more, if you can only convince them?

costs: Time off on its own, free to roam

movesNote.startingMovesNote: You start with Home on the Range, any moves from your Background, plus 1 of your choice.

name: The Seeker
description: Look at us. Huddling behind our walls, hearing evil in every passing noise. Cowards, all. All, but you. You fear not the unknown. You plunge into it, searching. Grasping at what has been lost. What will you find, o Seeker? Signs of a bright new age? Or signs of our doom?
statsNote: Assign these scores to your stats: +2, +1, +1, +0, +0, -1

backgrounds.label: Patriot
backgrounds.description:
These people are family. Chaos grows all around, but you'll be damned if you'll let your family come to harm. Damned indeed.

You have sought out and embraced dark power to protect that which you hold dear. Or perhaps that power fell upon you, and you took it up for the greater good. Either way, you seek more.

You start with the Let's Make a Deal move and are Well Versed in the Things Below. Go mark them now. You've also acquired 1 major arcanum.

backgrounds.label: Antiquarian
backgrounds.description:
The past has buried many secrets, and you are determined to dig them up. Years of study across the land have led you here, and you are convinced that this town holds the key to your greatest discoveries. What is it you hope to find? What is it that keeps you here?

Your travels and studies mean that you start with the Polyglot move and are Well Versed in the Makers and their arts. Go mark them now. You've also acquired 1 major arcanum.

backgrounds.label: Witch Hunter
backgrounds.description:
You've dedicated your life to rooting out and destroying horrors and their servants. What set you down this path? What did you sacrifice to walk it? What led you to call Stonetop home?

Regardless, you start with the Everything Bleeds move and are Well Versed in (pick 1) the Fae, the Things Below, or the Last Door and what lies beyond. Go mark them now. You've also acquired 1 major arcanum.

instincts.description: To scheme, manipulate, and plot.
To seek answers that maybe you oughtn't.
To assume you know best, that you can't fail.
To avoid straight answers; to keep secrets.
To think big and pursue grandiose goals.

origin.region: Lygos or some other point south

specialPossessions.pickNote: Pick 2, in addition to your scribe's tools

options.label: Scribe's tools
options.description: parchment, ink, pigments, vials, quills, ◇ a notebook, etc.

options.label: Books & scrolls
options.description: (○○○○○ uses) a collection of arcane texts and forbidden lore. Mark a use to Know Things about the topic at hand as if you rolled a 10+.

options.label: Distillery
options.description: (○○ uses) alchemical distillation equipment, reagents, solvents, tubes, flasks; also produces fine spirits (Value 1).

options.label: Engineer's tools
options.description: measuring string, stakes, levels, plumb bobs, ◇ compasses, ◇ drawing tools, plans, etc.

options.label: Laboratory
options.description: retorts, tubes, alembics, crucibles, ◇ a furnace, notebooks full of notes; a workspace for research, preparation, and experimentation.

options.label: Paraphernalia
options.description: chalk, candles, inks, salts, herbs, ◇ a ritual knife, ◇ a compass, etc.

options.label: Trade contacts
options.description: small amounts of salt, glass, silk, spice, medicinal herbs, pigments, ivory, etc.

lore.title: Collection
lore.description: In your travels and investigations you have acquired arcana—artifacts of power and mystery. Your Background grants you 1 major arcanum. Answer at least 2 questions about it.

lore.title: Major Arcanum
lore.description:
Your Background grants you 1 major arcanum.

Answer at least 2 questions about it:

options.description: Where did you acquire it?
From whose grasp did you wrest it?
Who else wants it?
What did it cost you?

You’ve begun to unlock the mysteries of your major arcanum; mark 1 or on the front of its insert.

When and how did that happen?

lore.title: Minor Arcana
lore.description: Ask the GM for the minor arcana cards. Draw 3 at random and review both sides.

options.description: Choose one whose secrets you have unlocked. If it's portable, keep it on your person or hidden away. Where is it now? How did you come to master it?
Choose another that you have not yet mastered. It is either in your possession or in a secret place known only to you. Where is it? How did you find it?
The third you have not yet found, but you have a lead on it. Give the card back to the GM but make note of it here. What do you know about it?

movesNote.startingMovesNote: You start with Well Versed, Work With What You’ve Got, plus 1 from your Background.

name: The Would-Be Hero
description: Most people hope for a quiet life. They spend their days a-worrying: about a leaky roof, a sick child, their crops. But you aren't like most people—you're on a different path. A path to adventure! There's greatness in you. Let's hope you live long enough for everyone else to see it.
statsNote: Assign these scores to your stats: +1, +0, +0, +0, +0, -1

backgrounds.label: Impetuous Youth
backgrounds.description:
Stonetop has always been home, but you chafe at the demands of mundane life and have always longed for more. Excitement! Danger!

When you make a move and come up short, you can give it your all and turn a 6- into a 7-9, a 7-9 into a 10+, and (if it matters) a 10-11 into a 12+. But if you do, pick 1 (the GM will fill in the details): you get hurt (2d4 damage and an actual injury); you cause collateral damage, endanger others, or otherwise escalate the situation; or something on your person is lost or breaks.

backgrounds.label: Driven
backgrounds.description:
You once led a simple life, but something happened. Something changed you, burdened you with terrible purpose. What was it? Choose 1: a loved one was killed or abducted; someone gave their life to save you; your idol sacrificed themselves to save many; you stumbled upon a dark mystery; or you must make amends for a terrible mistake.

You always have the option to Burn Brightly; you can spend 2 XP after you roll to add +1, even if you don't have enough XP to level.

backgrounds.label: Destined
backgrounds.description:
Fate has laid her hand upon you. Choose 3-4 items to describe your destiny.

At the start of a session, roll +Omens: on a 7+, lose all Omens and the GM will share a vision or portent that points toward your fate; also, on a 10+, ask the GM a follow-up question and get a clear, helpful answer; on a 6-, don't mark XP, hold +1 Omen, and tell us of your recent nightmares or a troubling vision.

Until your destiny is fulfilled, treat a 6- on Death's Door as a 7-9, and a 7-9 as a 10+.

instincts.description: To refuse to back down, give up, give in.
To question yourself, your actions, your worth.
To prove yourself, to yourself and others.
To assume the best, and that things are simple.
To put the needs/wants of others above your own.

origin.region: Lygos or some other point south

options.label: A good dog
options.description: a loyal follower; HP 6; Damage d4 (hand, harmless); Instinct: to guard & protect; Cost: love and care.

options.label: Heap of expectations
options.description: the hopes and dreams of all who believe in you. Of little practical use, but it's there.

options.label: Husbandry tools
options.description: brushes, muzzles, collars, feed, ◇ whips, ◇ bridles, etc. Gain advantage to Persuade domestic beasts (livestock, dogs, etc.).

options.label: Personal token
options.description: something that marks your place as a would-be hero.

options.label: A family heirloom (reroll one failed roll per session)
A lucky charm (mark it; clear when you Make Camp; while marked, +1 forward)
A trophy from a past victory
Smithy
options.description: (or access to it): iron goods, ingots, thick gloves, ◇ tongs, ◇ bellows, an anvil, etc.

options.label: Stoneworker's tools
options.description: chisels, drills, ◇ prybars, ◇ spikes, ◇ block & tackles, wheelbarrow, etc.

options.label: Tannery
options.description: (or access to it): hides, pelts, alum, salt, ◇ frames, ◇ knives; leather goods, straps, armor, etc.
Fire, burning, the smell of charred flesh.
That they won't take you seriously.
That you really aren't cut out for this.
The death of your family or loved ones.
Being alone and helpless.
Violence, bloodshed, and pain.
Monsters.
What you're capable of.
What you must do.

lore.title: What Makes You Burn with Righteous Anger?

options.description: Bullying, slavery, and oppression.
Wanton cruelty and unnecessary suffering.
Injustice and inequality.
Cowardice, treachery, and selfishness.
The despoiling of beauty and innocence.
Threats to your loved ones.
Violence to children, animals, the innocent.
Perversions of nature.

lore.title: When Did Your Fear or Anger Last Cause You Trouble?

options.description: When did your fear or anger last cause you trouble?
What did you do?
How did it turn out?

movesNote.startingMovesNote: You start with Anger is a Gift, Potential for Greatness, and 2 other moves of your choice.
